target.name: Chai Discovery
target.founder: Joshua Meier (CEO, ex-Chief AI Officer at Absci, ex-Senior Researcher at Meta/FAIR co-leading ESM/protein language modeling; CEO of Provita Pharma at age 16; Harvard CS), Jack Dent (President, met Meier first day of Harvard CS classes; previously led product/engineering teams at Stripe)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Chai Discovery is a Series B biotech company founded by Joshua Meier (former Chief AI Officer at Absci and co-leader of protein language modeling at Meta/FAIR) and Jack Dent (ex-Stripe). The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public-market biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers, with 4 direct comparables at Series B: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression, using the $1.3B last post-money as a base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-stage biotech comparables yields $1.3B, the midpoint of a $600M–$3.3B range. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.7x across the three public names) cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed. These two methods triangulate to a low of $1.1B, median of $1.9B, and high of $2.8B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B–$2.8B, centered near $1.9B. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (precluding public-multiple anchoring) and wide dispersion in peer valuations ($600M–$3.3B), reflecting early-stage heterogeneity in biotech development progress and capital efficiency.
